repo.description: The most widely used Python to C compiler
repo.name: cython

body:
160->152 bytes (-5%) on Linux AMD64

I considered bit fields because all three attributes are flags, but they don't make a difference due to struct alignment.

I also took the liberty to remove some old comments while at it that simply refer to attributes being implemented differently in CPython and Cython – useless information at this point.

comment_data.body: I think this is still fine. The documentation of `T_BOOL` says that it should point to a `char` anyway. Unless there's good reason to want to implement it as a function?
Ha, right. I had actually tried bit fields and various field reorderings and this was a left-over. I'll revert that part.

… we might be fixing a Schrödingbug on big endian machines here then, right?

EDIT: The tests only use the flag attribute once, checking that it's 0 when it's _not_ running, which doesn't preclude the case that it's _always_ 0.

https://github.com/cython/cython/blob/072985c5cf33d0824853602be063e08e9821b203/tests/run/test_asyncgen.py#L531

I'll use the function implementation as an untested fix for 3.2.x because I noticed that there are a lot of issues in master that need proper 3.3-level fixing following CPython.

3.2.x fix is in https://github.com/cython/cython/commit/86ba58478a62ed8c3ef50ff402a673e86318fa18
PR for 3.3 is https://github.com/cython/cython/pull/7777

IBM do provide free Github actions runners for s390x: https://github.com/IBM/actionspz/

I have no idea how much of an effort it is to set up, or how quick it runs, (or anything else). But if we wanted to add a big-endian test then it's probably the easiest way of doing it.  I don't know of any other semi-commonly used big-endian systems.
(I'm not really suggesting we should do add s390x tests. Just saying "it's there")

We've had a few big-endian issues in the past and only became aware of them because someone else noticed. We should notice them ourselves. I've asked for a runner.

https://github.com/IBM/actionspz/issues/121

commit.message: Make AsyncGenObject 8 bytes smaller (5%, 160->152 bytes on Linux AMD64).

milestone.description:
Main features:
- Pattern matching
- Generics / subscripted container types
- `@cython.collection_type()`
- `restrict`
- more vectorcalls
- more thread safety
- smaller modules, more shared code
- remove Py3.8 support, keep Py3.9+

title: Make AsyncGenObject 8 bytes smaller
